response: The internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense networking research, and ARPANET was first demonstrated in 1969 with a message sent from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. A major turning point came in 1983 with the adoption of TCP/IP, which laid the technical foundation for the modern internet, followed by Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 and the launch of the Mosaic browser in 1993, which brought the web to ordinary users. The main argument is that the internet evolved through key technical breakthroughs and commercial waves—from the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash to the rise of social media, smartphones after the iPhone’s 2007 launch, and cloud computing with AWS in 2006. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and supports nearly every part of modern life, including communication, commerce, and entertainment.
